commands.name: /clear
commands.description: Wipe conversation history.

commands.name: /compact [focus]
commands.description: Summarize context to reclaim tokens while preserving thread. Use at ~80% context.

commands.name: /resume [id]
commands.description: Resume a past session.

commands.name: /rename [name]
commands.description: Rename the current session for easier recall.

commands.name: /branch [name]
commands.description: Fork conversation at this point — like git branch for your session.

commands.name: /rewind
commands.description: Roll back conversation AND code changes to an earlier checkpoint.

commands.name: /export [file]
commands.description: Export full conversation as text file.

commands.name: /copy [N]
commands.description: Copy last (or Nth) response to clipboard.

commands.name: /exit
commands.description: Exit Claude Code.

commands.name: /context
commands.description: Visual grid showing context window consumption — what's using space and how much remains.

commands.name: /usage
commands.description: Show plan usage and rate limits.

commands.name: /cost
commands.description: Token usage and estimated cost for current session.

commands.name: /diff
commands.description: Interactive diff viewer — uncommitted git changes + Claude's per-turn changes.

commands.name: /doctor
commands.description: Diagnose installation, settings, and environment issues.

commands.name: /insights
commands.description: Analyze session patterns and identify friction points.

commands.name: /help
commands.description: List all available commands including custom ones.

commands.name: /release-notes
commands.description: View full changelog for current version.

commands.name: /pr-comments [PR]
commands.description: Fetch GitHub PR review comments into context.

commands.name: /model [name]
commands.description: Switch model mid-session. Arrow keys adjust effort level in picker.

commands.name: /effort [level]
commands.description: Set reasoning depth: `low` `medium` `high` `max` `auto`. `max` = Opus 4.6 deep think.

commands.name: /fast [on|off]
commands.description: Speed-optimized API settings (same model, faster params). Great for rapid iteration.

commands.name: /plan [desc]
commands.description: Read-only reasoning mode — Claude analyzes and plans without executing changes.

commands.name: /sandbox
commands.description: Toggle sandboxed execution for risky operations.

commands.name: /config
commands.description: Open settings UI (theme, model prefs, output style).

commands.name: /permissions
commands.description: Manage allow / ask / deny rules at project/global scope.

commands.name: /memory
commands.description: Edit CLAUDE.md and auto-memory. Claude saves build commands, debugging insights, conventions.

commands.name: /init
commands.description: Bootstrap project with a CLAUDE.md file for coding standards and context.

commands.name: /hooks
commands.description: View lifecycle hooks — shell commands that run before/after Claude actions.

commands.name: /keybindings
commands.description: Open keybinding configuration file.

commands.name: /login / /logout
commands.description: Sign in or out of Anthropic account.

commands.name: /privacy-settings
commands.description: Update privacy preferences (Pro/Max plans).

commands.name: /extra-usage
commands.description: Configure pay-as-you-go overflow when plan limits hit.

commands.name: /color [color]
commands.description: Set prompt bar color — visually distinguish parallel sessions.

commands.name: /mcp
commands.description: Manage MCP server connections. Servers expose tools as `/mcp__<server>__<prompt>` commands.

commands.name: /plugin
commands.description: Manage plugins — install, remove, discover from marketplaces.

commands.name: /reload-plugins
commands.description: Hot-reload all active plugins without restarting.

commands.name: /agents
commands.description: Manage sub-agent configurations.

commands.name: /chrome
commands.description: Configure browser automation integration.

commands.name: /ide
commands.description: Manage IDE integrations (VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains).

commands.name: /install-github-app
commands.description: Set up Claude GitHub App for PR reviews via GitHub Actions.

commands.name: /install-slack-app
commands.description: Install Claude for Slack integration.

commands.name: /add-dir <path>
commands.description: Add an additional working directory for this session.

commands.name: /desktop
commands.description: Open session in the Desktop app.

commands.name: /remote-control
commands.description: Enable remote control from claude.ai.

commands.name: /simplify
commands.description: 3-agent parallel code review. Replaced the older `/review` command.

commands.name: /batch <task>
commands.description: Run a task across multiple files simultaneously.

commands.name: /debug
commands.description: Structured debugging workflow — reads errors, proposes fixes, tests iteratively.

commands.name: /loop [interval] <prompt>
commands.description: Recurring prompt on a schedule. E.g., `/loop 5m check deploy status`. Units: `s m h d`.

commands.name: /schedule [desc]
commands.description: Create cloud-hosted scheduled task (persists beyond session).

commands.name: /security-review
commands.description: Scan current branch diff for vulnerabilities (SQLi, XSS, exposed creds).

commands.name: /btw <question>
commands.description: Quick side question — no context cost, keeps main conversation clean.

commands.name: /claude-api
commands.description: Quick reference for Anthropic API usage patterns.

commands.name: Agent Teams
commands.description: Enable with `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1`. Lead agent spawns teammates, coordinates via shared task list.

commands.name: /agents
commands.description: View and manage sub-agent configurations. Define in `.claude/agents/`.

commands.name: context: fork
commands.description: Skill frontmatter — runs skill in a forked sub-agent to avoid polluting main context.

commands.name: Agent SDK
commands.description: `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` — Full control over orchestration, tools, and permissions.

commands.name: Builder-Validator
commands.description: Pattern: one agent builds, another validates. Exploration → B-V for implementation.

commands.name: Parallel Sessions
commands.description: Multiple `claude` instances in different terminals/worktrees for true parallelism.

commands.name: claude -p "prompt"
commands.description: Non-interactive single prompt. Scriptable for CI, cron, and automation.

commands.name: --output-format json
commands.description: Machine-readable output. Options: `json` `stream-json` `text`

commands.name: --model <name>
commands.description: Choose model at launch.

commands.name: --dangerously-skip-permissions
commands.description: No approval prompts. CI/cron only — scope prompts tightly.

commands.name: --allowedTools "Bash,Read"
commands.description: Pre-approve specific tools. More granular than skip-permissions.

commands.name: --permission-mode acceptEdits
commands.description: Auto-accept file writes, still prompt for other actions.

commands.name: --append-system-prompt
commands.description: Add to system prompt (preserves built-ins). Preferred for most use cases.

commands.name: --system-prompt-file f.md
commands.description: Replace system prompt entirely from a file.

commands.name: --continue / --resume <id>
commands.description: Resume most recent or specific session by ID.

commands.name: --bare
commands.description: Skip hooks, skills, MCP, plugins, auto-memory. Clean for CI.

commands.name: --json-schema '{...}'
commands.description: Enforce structured output schema on response.

commands.name: Shift + Tab
commands.description: Cycle modes: normal → auto-accept → plan. The most-used shortcut.

commands.name: Ctrl + O
commands.description: Toggle verbose / extended thinking output.

commands.name: Ctrl + R
commands.description: Reverse search command history.

commands.name: Ctrl + F × 2
commands.description: Kill all background agents (press twice rapidly).

commands.name: Esc
commands.description: Cancel current generation or exit menu.

commands.name: @ file
commands.description: Attach file or directory to context inline.

commands.name: ! command
commands.description: Run shell command with conversation context. E.g., `! git status`

commands.name: /vim
commands.description: Enable vim keybindings in the prompt editor.

commands.name: ~/.claude/skills/
commands.description: Personal skills — available across all projects. Add `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter.

commands.name: .claude/skills/
commands.description: Project skills — shared via git with your team.

commands.name: .claude/commands/
commands.description: Legacy commands — still work. Skills recommended for new commands.

commands.name: allowed-tools
commands.description: Frontmatter: grant tool access without per-use approval. E.g., `Bash(git add:*)`

commands.name: model: haiku
commands.description: Frontmatter: override model per skill. Use `haiku` for cheap repetitive tasks.

commands.name: context: fork
commands.description: Frontmatter: run in a sub-agent so it doesn't pollute main context.

commands.name: $ARGUMENTS
commands.description: Capture args passed after the command. `/fix-issue 123 high` → `"123 high"`

commands.name: disable-model-invocation
commands.description: Frontmatter: prevent auto-invocation. Explicit `/command` only.

commands.name: Decompose
commands.description: Split a complex goal into granular, executable steps with clear dependencies.

commands.name: Route
commands.description: Assign tasks to specialized sub-agents based on capability matching.

commands.name: Parallelize
commands.description: Run non-dependent tasks simultaneously to compress wall-clock time.

commands.name: Handoff
commands.description: Transfer full context between agents or back to a human operator.

commands.name: Synchronize
commands.description: Ensure all agents are working off the latest data version before proceeding.

commands.name: Replan
commands.description: Revise the task graph mid-execution when assumptions break or new info emerges.

commands.name: Checkpoint
commands.description: Save full state at a known-good point so you can roll back if a path fails.

commands.name: Backtrack
commands.description: Abandon a failing path and revert to the last checkpoint to try an alternative approach.

commands.name: Chain-of-Thought
commands.description: Document every logical step taken. Makes reasoning auditable and debuggable.

commands.name: ReAct Loop
commands.description: Iteratively Think → Act → Observe. The core agent execution cycle.

commands.name: Step-Back
commands.description: Identify core principles before specific execution. "What problem am I actually solving?"

commands.name: Self-Consistency
commands.description: Generate multiple solutions via different reasoning paths and pick the most common/best one.

commands.name: Reflect
commands.description: Critically analyze the previous output for errors, gaps, or unstated assumptions.

commands.name: Audit
commands.description: Verify output against strict compliance, security, or data rules.

commands.name: Validate
commands.description: Fact-check against a "Ground Truth" source — docs, tests, schema, or API response.

commands.name: Refactor
commands.description: Rewrite logic for better performance or clarity — not just patching symptoms.

commands.name: Stress-Test
commands.description: Find the breaking point of a plan, code path, or architecture under edge cases.

commands.name: Remediate
commands.description: Address the root cause of an identified bug, not just the surface error.

commands.name: Ground
commands.description: Restrict responses to provided reference materials only — no hallucination allowed.

commands.name: Synthesize
commands.description: Create a "State of the Project" summary from raw, scattered data sources.

commands.name: Prune
commands.description: Clear outdated context to focus the agent's attention on what's current.

commands.name: Commit
commands.description: Store key results in long-term persistent memory for future sessions.

commands.name: Retrieve
commands.description: RAG-style lookup from a knowledge base, vector store, or document corpus.

commands.name: Search
commands.description: Web or codebase search to fill knowledge gaps the agent can't answer from memory.

commands.name: Inspect
commands.description: Read the current state of the environment — filesystem, database, API response, logs.

commands.name: Attach
commands.description: Pull specific artifacts (files, docs, images) into the working context on demand.

commands.name: Invoke
commands.description: Call an external tool, API, or function and parse the structured result.

commands.name: Observe
commands.description: Read the environment state after an action — the "O" in ReAct, independently named.

commands.name: Sandbox
commands.description: Execute code or commands in an isolated environment to prevent unintended side effects.

commands.name: Escalate
commands.description: Recognize when a task exceeds the agent's capability and surface it to a human or higher-privilege system.

commands.name: Transform
commands.description: Convert data between formats (JSON→XML, CSV→SQL, Mermaid→image) as an intermediate step.

commands.name: Retry
commands.description: Re-execute a failed tool call with adjusted parameters or after a backoff delay.

commands.name: Constrain
commands.description: Enforce boundaries on what the agent is allowed to do — tool permissions, token budgets, scope limits.

commands.name: Gate
commands.description: Require explicit human approval before irreversible or high-risk actions.

commands.name: Abort
commands.description: Hard-stop execution when a safety threshold, cost limit, or error budget is breached.

commands.name: Throttle
commands.description: Rate-limit agent actions to prevent runaway loops, API exhaustion, or cost overruns.

commands.name: Scope
commands.description: Restrict the agent's view to only the files, data, or systems relevant to the current task.

commands.name: Fence
commands.description: Define explicit "never do this" rules — immutable constraints the agent cannot override.

commands.name: Emit
commands.description: Produce a structured output artifact — JSON, file, diff, commit, PR, or API response.

commands.name: Summarize
commands.description: Compress a long working session into a concise deliverable for the human.

commands.name: Present
commands.description: Format and deliver results in context-appropriate form — diff, report, Slack message, dashboard.

commands.name: Narrate
commands.description: Provide a running explanation of what the agent is doing and why — builds trust and debuggability.

commands.name: Log
commands.description: Record actions, decisions, and outcomes for later audit without cluttering the main output.

commands.name: Delegate
commands.description: Assign a scoped sub-task to a sub-agent with its own context fork. Distinct from Route (which chooses the agent).

commands.name: Merge
commands.description: Combine outputs from parallel agents into a single coherent result.

commands.name: Negotiate
commands.description: Agents resolve conflicting outputs or approaches through structured debate.

commands.name: Vote
commands.description: Ensemble pattern — multiple agents weigh in and the majority/best answer wins.

commands.name: Supervise
commands.description: A lead agent monitors sub-agents, intervening only when they stall, conflict, or exceed bounds.

commands.name: Broadcast
commands.description: Push a state update, constraint change, or new context to all active agents simultaneously.

commands.name: Spec
commands.description: Define acceptance criteria, constraints, and deliverables before any work begins. The contract between the human and the foreman.

commands.name: Sequence
commands.description: Order tasks by dependency graph — what blocks what — not just priority. Produces a DAG (directed acyclic graph) of execution.

commands.name: Estimate
commands.description: Size each task (time, tokens, complexity) so the foreman knows when something is running long and needs intervention.

commands.name: Assign
commands.description: Match a task to the best-fit agent based on capability, current load, and context proximity.

commands.name: Rebalance
commands.description: Redistribute work across agents when one finishes early, another gets stuck, or a new dependency emerges.

commands.name: Phase
commands.description: Group tasks into ordered stages (e.g., scaffold → implement → test → integrate). The next phase doesn't start until the current one passes its gate.

commands.name: Track
commands.description: Maintain a persistent task board with lifecycle states: `queued → assigned → in-progress → review → done → blocked`.

commands.name: Poll
commands.description: Periodically check agent status rather than waiting for callbacks. Detects silent failures and stalls.

commands.name: Unblock
commands.description: Detect and resolve blockers — reassign the task, provide missing context, or escalate to the human.

commands.name: Promote
commands.description: Move a task through its lifecycle gates after validation passes (e.g., in-progress → review after tests pass).

commands.name: Flag
commands.description: Mark a task or agent output as needing human attention without halting the entire pipeline.

commands.name: Deprioritize
commands.description: Push a non-critical blocked task to the back of the queue so other work continues flowing.

commands.name: Spawn
commands.description: Create a new agent with scoped context, tool permissions, and a specific task assignment.

commands.name: Heartbeat
commands.description: Verify an agent is still alive and making progress — not spinning, looping, or silently failing.

commands.name: Reassign
commands.description: Take work from a stalled or failing agent and give it to another (with context transfer).

commands.name: Retire
commands.description: Clean up an agent when its work is done — reclaim context budget, save outputs, update the board.

commands.name: Quarantine
commands.description: Isolate an agent producing bad outputs so its work doesn't contaminate other agents' context.

commands.name: Converge
commands.description: All parallel streams merge back to a single coherent state — code, docs, configs unified.

commands.name: Reconcile
commands.description: Resolve conflicts between agents that modified overlapping files, APIs, or shared state.

commands.name: Accept
commands.description: The foreman validates that acceptance criteria from the Spec are met. Binary: pass or fail.

commands.name: Integrate
commands.description: Run integration tests, lint, type-check — verify that individually-correct pieces work together.

commands.name: Ship
commands.description: Final assembly — combine all outputs, produce the deliverable (PR, artifact, deployment), close the board.

commands.name: Retrospect
commands.description: Post-completion analysis: what worked, what stalled, which estimates were off. Feed learnings back into memory for next time.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Spec
commands.description: Write acceptance criteria in CLAUDE.md or a `spec.md` file the foreman references.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Sequence / Phase
commands.description: Encode the DAG as a checklist or task list in a markdown file the foreman maintains.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Spawn
commands.description: Agent Teams auto-spawns, or manual parallel `claude` sessions in separate worktrees.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Track
commands.description: Foreman maintains a `BOARD.md` or structured JSON file, updating after each Poll cycle.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Poll / Heartbeat
commands.description: `/loop` with a status-check prompt, or the foreman's own ReAct loop reading agent output files.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Assign / Reassign
commands.description: Agent Teams handles this via the shared task list. Manual: write task to agent's input file.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Reconcile
commands.description: `git merge` + foreman reviews conflicts, or foreman runs `/diff` across branches.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Accept
commands.description: Foreman runs the test suite and compares output against the Spec's acceptance criteria.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Ship
commands.description: Custom `/ship` skill: run tests → commit → push → open PR.

commands.section: Mapping Foreman Commands to Claude Code
commands.name: Retrospect
commands.description: `/compact` the session with a focus on "what worked and what didn't" → `/memory` to persist.

commands.name: Timeout
commands.description: Agent exceeded its time or token budget. Distinct from Abort (intentional) — Timeout is the system catching a runaway.

commands.name: Fallback
commands.description: When the primary approach fails, switch to a degraded but functional alternative (e.g., Opus fails → retry with Sonnet).

commands.name: Replay
commands.description: Re-run a failed sequence from a checkpoint with modified parameters. Distinct from Retry (single tool call) — Replay re-executes a full sub-workflow.

commands.name: Degrade
commands.description: Reduce scope or quality to keep the pipeline moving when a component is unavailable (e.g., skip optional validation, use cached data).

commands.name: Dead Letter
commands.description: Capture failed tasks with their full context (inputs, state, error) into a holding queue for later diagnosis and manual retry.

commands.name: Circuit Break
commands.description: After N consecutive failures on the same operation, stop retrying and fail fast. Prevents infinite retry loops and token burn.

commands.name: Compensate
commands.description: Undo the side effects of a partially-completed sequence that failed midway (e.g., rollback a DB migration, revert a partial commit).

commands.name: Window
commands.description: Actively manage what's in the agent's context window — what gets included, summarized, or dropped at any given moment.

commands.name: Inject
commands.description: Push specific knowledge into an agent's context right before it needs it — just-in-time context, not load-everything-upfront.

commands.name: Compress
commands.description: Reduce context size without losing critical information. Different from Prune (which drops things) — Compress keeps everything but smaller.

commands.name: Isolate
commands.description: Prevent context contamination between tasks. One agent's debugging output shouldn't pollute another agent's clean working context.

commands.name: Hydrate
commands.description: Reconstruct full context from a checkpoint or summary when resuming work. The inverse of Compress — expand a compressed state back to working detail.

commands.name: Prefill
commands.description: Pre-populate an agent's context with structured background (architecture docs, API schemas, conventions) before it receives its task.

commands.name: Evict
commands.description: Forcibly remove a specific piece of context that's causing confusion or anchoring the agent to a wrong approach. More surgical than Prune.

commands.name: Snapshot
commands.description: Capture the exact context state at a point in time for reproducibility — if you need to debug why an agent made a decision, replay from the snapshot.

commands.name: Trace
commands.description: Follow the full execution path across agents — which agent touched what, in what order, with what inputs and outputs.

commands.name: Diff State
commands.description: Compare the state at two points in the pipeline to find where things diverged from expectations.

commands.name: Replay Dry
commands.description: Re-run a sequence in read-only mode to observe behavior without side effects. For understanding, not fixing.

commands.name: Cost Audit
commands.description: Break down token/time/money spent per agent, per task, per phase. Essential for optimization and budget enforcement.

commands.name: Blame
commands.description: Given a bad output, trace back to the specific agent and decision that caused it — like `git blame` for agent pipelines.

commands.name: Profile
commands.description: Identify bottlenecks — which agents are slow, which tasks consume disproportionate context, where the pipeline stalls.

commands.name: Canary
commands.description: Run a new agent configuration on a small subset of tasks before rolling it out to the full pipeline. Catches regressions early.

commands.name: Assert
commands.description: Place runtime assertions at key points in the pipeline — "after this step, X should be true" — and halt if they fail.

commands.name: Message
commands.description: Direct agent-to-agent communication — structured request/response between two specific agents.

commands.name: Queue
commands.description: Async task queue that agents pull from. Decouples producers from consumers — agents work at their own pace.

commands.name: Publish / Subscribe
commands.description: Agents subscribe to event types and react when relevant events fire. Looser coupling than Broadcast (which pushes to all).

commands.name: Shared State
commands.description: A mutable data structure multiple agents can read and write — the BOARD.md / task-list pattern from the foreman section.

commands.name: Contract
commands.description: Define the expected input/output schema between agents so they can work independently without tight coupling.

commands.name: Pipe
commands.description: Chain agent outputs directly into the next agent's input — like Unix pipes for agent workflows.

commands.name: Barrier
commands.description: A synchronization point where all agents must arrive before any can proceed. Enforces phase boundaries.

commands.name: Mailbox
commands.description: Per-agent inbox that accumulates messages while the agent is busy. Agent processes them when it's ready, not when they arrive.

commands.name: Least Privilege
commands.description: Each agent gets only the permissions it needs for its specific task — no shared superuser context.

commands.name: Audit Trail
commands.description: Immutable, append-only log of every action every agent took — who did what, when, with what inputs. For compliance and forensics.

commands.name: Secret Scope
commands.description: Secrets are injected per-agent, not shared globally. An agent building a UI never sees the CyberArk credentials the provisioning agent uses.

commands.name: Trust Boundary
commands.description: Define which agents can communicate with which, and what data is allowed to cross boundaries.

commands.name: Attest
commands.description: An agent proves its output hasn't been tampered with — integrity verification for multi-agent chains (e.g., hash of output + agent ID).

commands.name: Redact
commands.description: Strip sensitive data from agent outputs before they're passed to other agents or logged. PII, secrets, tokens never leak downstream.

commands.name: Sandbox Escape Check
commands.description: Verify that a sandboxed agent hasn't broken out of its execution boundary — file access, network calls, privilege escalation.

commands.name: Provenance
commands.description: Tag every output with its origin — which agent, which model, which version, which inputs produced it. Chain of custody for generated artifacts.

commands.name: Rotate
commands.description: Periodically rotate credentials, tokens, and API keys used by long-running agents. Prevents stale credential accumulation.

commands.name: Template
commands.description: Reusable prompt structures with variable slots. Your custom skills in `~/.claude/skills/` are exactly this pattern.

commands.name: Persona
commands.description: Assign a role/expertise frame to shape an agent's reasoning — "you are a SailPoint IIQ expert" vs "you are a security auditor."

commands.name: Constrain Output
commands.description: Specify format, length, and structure of the response — JSON schema, Given/When/Then, Mermaid, XML, table.

commands.name: Few-Shot
commands.description: Provide examples of correct input→output pairs to calibrate behavior. 2-3 examples dramatically improve consistency.

commands.name: System Prompt
commands.description: The persistent instructions that frame every interaction. CLAUDE.md is literally this — project-wide System Prompt.

commands.name: Chain Prompt
commands.description: Break a complex prompt into sequential stages where each stage's output feeds the next. Reduces hallucination in long-reasoning tasks.

commands.name: Negative Prompt
commands.description: Explicitly state what the agent should NOT do. "Do not modify any files outside `/src`." "Do not use deprecated API v1 endpoints."

commands.name: Meta-Prompt
commands.description: A prompt that generates other prompts — "write me a SKILL.md that does X." Used to bootstrap new skills and custom commands.

commands.name: Calibrate
commands.description: Test a prompt against known-good inputs to verify it produces expected outputs before deploying it in a pipeline.

commands.name: Review
commands.description: Human inspects output before it progresses — async, non-blocking. Work continues on other tasks while review is pending.

commands.name: Approve
commands.description: Human gives explicit go/no-go — blocking. Pipeline halts until the human responds. Use sparingly.

commands.name: Steer
commands.description: Human provides mid-flight course correction without restarting — "actually, use the v2 API instead."

commands.name: Override
commands.description: Human manually replaces an agent's output with their own. The pipeline continues with the human's version.

commands.name: Annotate
commands.description: Human adds notes or context to agent output for downstream agents to consume. Enriches without replacing.

commands.name: Triage
commands.description: Human reviews a batch of flagged items and decides disposition for each — approve, reject, reassign, defer.

commands.name: Teach
commands.description: Human corrects an agent's mistake AND updates the persistent instructions (CLAUDE.md, skill file) so it doesn't recur.

commands.name: Delegate Back
commands.description: Human returns a task to the agent with additional context or constraints after reviewing it — "try again, but consider X."

commands.name: Elicit
commands.description: Gather requirements from stakeholders, tickets, or existing systems. Ingest ServiceNow RITMs, Jira issues, Slack threads, or user interviews into structured requirements.

commands.name: Acceptance Criteria
commands.description: Formalize pass/fail conditions — Given/When/Then, input/output pairs, or constraint lists. The contract between "what we want" and "how we know it's done."

commands.name: Spike
commands.description: Time-boxed exploration to reduce uncertainty before committing to an approach. "Can we even connect to this API? What does the response look like?"

commands.name: ADR
commands.description: Architecture Decision Record — capture the *why* behind a design choice so future agents and humans don't unknowingly undo it. Immutable once written.

commands.name: Threat Model
commands.description: Identify attack surfaces, trust boundaries, and security risks before implementation — not after. STRIDE, DREAD, or lightweight asset-based approaches.

commands.name: RFC
commands.description: Request for Comments — propose a significant change with context, alternatives considered, and trade-offs. Agents or humans review and approve before work begins.

commands.name: Scaffold
commands.description: Generate project or module boilerplate — directory structure, configs, base classes, CI templates. The skeleton before the meat.

commands.name: Implement
commands.description: Write the production code. The core build step — one task, one component, one deliverable.

commands.name: Wire
commands.description: Connect components together — API integrations, dependency injection, config binding, import graphs. Distinct from Implement (which builds individual pieces).

commands.name: Mock
commands.description: Create fake endpoints, services, or data for development and testing before real dependencies are available. Stubs, fixtures, contract-based mocks.

commands.name: Migrate
commands.description: Schema changes, data transformations, version upgrades. Distinct from Implement because migrations have rollback requirements and must be idempotent.

commands.name: Generate
commands.description: Auto-produce derived artifacts from a source of truth — TypeScript types from a schema, API clients from OpenAPI specs, IIQ connectors from config templates.

commands.name: Unit Test
commands.description: Test individual functions or classes in isolation. Fast, focused, deterministic. The foundation of the test pyramid.

commands.name: Integration Test
commands.description: Test components working together — APIs calling databases, services calling services, connectors talking to endpoints.

commands.name: E2E Test
commands.description: Full workflow simulation from user input to final output. Slowest, most brittle, but highest confidence.

commands.name: Regression Test
commands.description: Verify that new changes didn't break existing functionality. Run the existing suite against the new code.

commands.name: Load Test
commands.description: Verify behavior under scale — concurrent users, large datasets, sustained throughput. JMeter, k6, Locust.

commands.name: Fuzz
commands.description: Throw random or malformed inputs at the system to find crashes, panics, and unhandled edge cases.

commands.name: Smoke Test
commands.description: Quick sanity check that the basic happy path works before running the full suite. First gate after deployment.

commands.name: Contract Test
commands.description: Verify that two services agree on their shared interface — request/response shapes, error codes, auth headers — without running both together.

commands.name: Version
commands.description: Tag a release with semantic versioning (major.minor.patch). Communicates the nature of changes to consumers.

commands.name: Package
commands.description: Bundle artifacts for deployment — Docker images, binaries, archives, Helm charts, IIQ deployment packages.

commands.name: Stage
commands.description: Deploy to a pre-production environment for final validation. Mirror of production with synthetic or sanitized data.

commands.name: Deploy
commands.description: Push to production. Blue-green, rolling, or canary strategy depending on risk tolerance.

commands.name: Rollback
commands.description: Revert a bad deployment to the previous known-good state. Distinct from Compensate (broader) — Rollback specifically targets deployment artifacts.

commands.name: Feature Flag
commands.description: Toggle new functionality on/off without redeploying. Decouple deployment from release — ship dark, enable when ready.

commands.name: Canary Deploy
commands.description: Route a percentage of traffic to the new version before full rollout. Catch production-only issues with limited blast radius.

commands.name: Promote
commands.description: Move an artifact from one environment to the next — dev → staging → production. Same artifact, different config.

commands.name: Monitor
commands.description: Ongoing health checks, alerting, SLA tracking. Detect drift from expected behavior before users report it.

commands.name: Incident
commands.description: Detect, classify, and respond to production issues. Severity levels, runbooks, escalation paths, blameless post-mortems.

commands.name: Hotfix
commands.description: Emergency patch applied outside the normal release cycle. Bypasses staging for speed, but must be backported to the main branch.

commands.name: Deprecate
commands.description: Mark functionality for removal with a migration path and timeline. Consumers get warnings before it disappears.

commands.name: Decommission
commands.description: Safely shut down a service, integration, or infrastructure. Cleanup DNS, secrets, access, monitoring, documentation. The inverse of Scaffold.

commands.name: Patch
commands.description: Apply targeted security or dependency updates. Smaller than a release, more structured than a hotfix. Often automated via Dependabot or Renovate.

commands.name: Document
commands.description: Generate or update technical docs — API specs, runbooks, architecture diagrams, config references. Living docs, not afterthoughts.

commands.name: Changelog
commands.description: Maintain a human-readable record of what changed and why per release. Audience: consumers of the system, not the builders.

commands.name: Onboard
commands.description: Produce context that lets a new agent or human pick up the project — the "getting started" package. README, setup guide, key decisions, known gotchas.

commands.name: Runbook
commands.description: Step-by-step procedure for a specific operational scenario — "how to rotate the EPIC_EPCS CyberArk credential" or "how to restart the IIQ provisioning service."

commands.name: Postmortem
commands.description: Structured analysis of an incident — timeline, root cause, contributing factors, action items. Blameless, focused on systemic improvement.

commands.name: Provision
commands.description: Create an identity, account, or entitlement in a target system — IIQ pushing to Epic, Entra ID app registrations, SCIM user creation.

commands.name: Deprovision
commands.description: Remove access — the inverse of Provision, with full audit trail and confirmation that access is actually gone.

commands.name: Entitle
commands.description: Grant a specific permission or role. Finer-grained than Provision (which creates the account) — Entitle adds capabilities to an existing identity.

commands.name: Revoke
commands.description: Remove a specific entitlement without deprovisioning the entire account. Surgical access removal.

commands.name: Certify
commands.description: Periodic access review — manager confirms "yes, this person still needs this access." Catch entitlement creep.

commands.name: Recertify
commands.description: Re-run certification after a change event — role change, org transfer, risk flag, or time-based trigger.

commands.name: Joiner-Mover-Leaver
commands.description: The lifecycle trigger — detect an HR event and fire the appropriate provision/modify/deprovision workflow automatically.

commands.name: Correlate
commands.description: Match an identity across systems — "is jsmith in AD the same person as j.smith in Epic?" Identity resolution across disparate sources.

commands.name: Reconcile Identity
commands.description: Detect drift between the authoritative source and target systems — orphaned accounts, entitlements that don't match policy, stale access.

commands.name: Aggregate
commands.description: Pull current account and entitlement data from a target system into the identity governance platform. The read before the reconcile.

commands.name: Role Mine
commands.description: Analyze existing entitlements to discover natural role clusters. Bottom-up role engineering from real access patterns.

commands.name: Vault
commands.description: Store a secret in a centralized secrets manager (CyberArk, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault) with access controls and audit logging.

commands.name: Checkout
commands.description: Retrieve a credential for time-limited use. CyberArk PSM pattern — credential is locked to the session and returned on completion.

commands.name: Rotate
commands.description: Scheduled credential rotation with zero-downtime swap — update the secret in the vault and all consumers without service interruption.

commands.name: Inject Secret
commands.description: Deliver a credential to a runtime without exposing it in config files or env vars — sidecar, init container, or API fetch at startup.

commands.name: Break Glass
commands.description: Emergency access to a privileged credential outside normal workflow. Audited, alerted, time-limited, requires justification.

commands.name: Seal
commands.description: Mark a secret as compromised or expired and ensure all cached copies are purged — revoke, rotate, and verify in one operation.

commands.name: Escrow
commands.description: Store a recovery key or credential with a trusted third party for disaster recovery — distinct from Vault (operational) vs Escrow (break-the-glass recovery).

commands.name: Bind
commands.description: Establish an authenticated connection to a directory — LDAP bind, Kerberos TGT request, OIDC token exchange. The handshake before any query.

commands.name: Query
commands.description: Search the directory for objects matching criteria — LDAP filters, IIQ Filter API, Graph API queries. The read operation.

commands.name: Enforce Policy
commands.description: Apply authentication or password policy to a scope — CyberArk auth policies, fine-grained password policies, conditional access rules.

commands.name: Sync
commands.description: Replicate identity data between systems — Azure Cross-Tenant Sync, AD Connect, SCIM provisioning cycles (~40-min cadence).

commands.name: Delegate Admin
commands.description: Grant administrative permissions over a specific OU or scope — OU delegation in AD, administrative units in Entra ID.

commands.name: Nest / Flatten
commands.description: Manage group membership hierarchy — nested group resolution for systems that support it, flattening for those that don't (Azure CTS).

commands.name: Claim
commands.description: Assert an identity attribute for consumption by a relying party — SAML assertion, OIDC claim, JWT claim mapping, token enrichment.

commands.name: Federate
commands.description: Establish trust between identity providers — SAML federation, OIDC trust, cross-domain B2B/B2C relationships.

commands.name: Step-Up Auth
commands.description: Require additional authentication factors when the risk level increases — MFA challenge on sensitive operations, adaptive auth.

commands.name: Segment
commands.description: Isolate network zones — microsegmentation, VLAN assignment, SDN rules, security groups. Limit lateral movement.

commands.name: Tunnel
commands.description: Establish encrypted connectivity — WireGuard, IPsec, Tailscale, SSH tunneling. Secure the transport layer.

commands.name: Attest Device
commands.description: Verify device health and posture before granting access — TPM attestation, MDM compliance, certificate validation, PCR verification.

commands.name: JIT Access
commands.description: Just-In-Time — grant access only when needed, revoke automatically after a time window or session end. Minimize standing privileges.

commands.name: Quarantine Host
commands.description: Isolate a compromised or non-compliant host from the network pending investigation. Infra-level variant of agent Quarantine (IX).

commands.name: Baseline
commands.description: Establish the "normal" behavior profile for a system, user, or network segment. Deviations from baseline trigger alerts.

commands.name: Harden
commands.description: Apply security configuration to infrastructure — CIS benchmarks, STIG compliance, disable unnecessary services, enforce TLS versions.

commands.name: Zero Trust Evaluate
commands.description: Continuous policy evaluation — re-assess trust for every request based on identity, device, location, behavior, and risk score. Never implicit trust.

commands.name: Collect Evidence
commands.description: Gather artifacts proving a control is operating — screenshots, logs, config exports, query results, attestation records.

commands.name: Attest Control
commands.description: Formally certify that a security control is in place and functioning. Your AD OU restriction and password policy findings are exactly this.

commands.name: Map Control
commands.description: Link a technical implementation to a compliance framework requirement — "this GPO satisfies HIPAA §164.312(d)." Traceability.

commands.name: Gap Analysis
commands.description: Compare current state against a target framework and identify what's missing. The delta between "where we are" and "where we need to be."

commands.name: Remediation Plan
commands.description: For each gap, define the fix, owner, timeline, and acceptance criteria. Your Given/When/Then AC patterns are textbook remediation plans.

commands.name: Exception
commands.description: Formally document and approve a deviation from policy — compensating controls, expiration date, risk acceptance, and sign-off.

commands.name: Continuous Compliance
commands.description: Automate evidence collection and control attestation on a schedule — not just point-in-time audits but ongoing verification.

commands.name: Ingest
commands.description: Collect logs, events, and telemetry from infrastructure into a SIEM or analysis platform. The raw data pipeline.

commands.name: Correlate Events
commands.description: Connect related signals across systems — failed login + VPN connection + file access = potential lateral movement.

commands.name: Triage Alert
commands.description: Evaluate an alert for severity, scope, and required response — true positive, false positive, or needs investigation.

commands.name: Contain
commands.description: Limit blast radius of an active threat — disable account, isolate host, block IP, revoke sessions, kill tokens.

commands.name: Eradicate
commands.description: Remove root cause of a security incident — patch vulnerability, remove malware, close unauthorized access path, rotate compromised creds.

commands.name: Recover
commands.description: Restore normal operations after an incident — re-enable accounts, restore from backup, validate integrity, resume monitoring.

commands.name: Hunt
commands.description: Proactive search for threats that haven't triggered alerts — hypothesis-driven investigation using logs, telemetry, and behavioral analysis.

commands.name: Outline
commands.description: Define the document's skeleton — sections, flow, argument structure — before writing any prose. The blueprint.

commands.name: Scope
commands.description: Explicitly state what the document covers and what it doesn't. Prevents scope creep mid-draft and sets reader expectations.

commands.name: Audience
commands.description: Identify who will read this and calibrate tone, depth, and assumed knowledge accordingly. An exec summary ≠ an engineering spec.

commands.name: Frame
commands.description: Choose the document's rhetorical structure — problem/solution, current-state/future-state, situation/complication/resolution, chronological, topical.

commands.name: Template
commands.description: Select or create a reusable document skeleton for a recurring deliverable type — finding reports, architecture proposals, user stories, runbooks.

commands.name: Draft
commands.description: Write the first pass — get ideas down without polishing. Prioritize completeness over perfection.

commands.name: Expand
commands.description: Take a bullet point, outline item, or stub and develop it into full prose with supporting detail.

commands.name: Condense
commands.description: Compress verbose content into tighter prose without losing meaning. The inverse of Expand.

commands.name: Translate Register
commands.description: Rewrite content for a different audience — technical to executive, internal to customer-facing, formal to conversational.

commands.name: Cite
commands.description: Add references, sources, or evidence to support claims. Link assertions to authoritative sources.

commands.name: Illustrate
commands.description: Add diagrams, tables, examples, or analogies that make abstract concepts concrete. Mermaid, sequence diagrams, architecture diagrams.

commands.name: Cross-Reference
commands.description: Link related sections, documents, or external resources so the reader can navigate the knowledge graph.

commands.name: Proofread
commands.description: Check for grammar, spelling, punctuation, and typographical errors. Mechanical correctness.

commands.name: Edit
commands.description: Improve clarity, flow, and structure. Rewrite awkward sentences, eliminate redundancy, strengthen transitions.

commands.name: Peer Review
commands.description: Have another agent or human read for technical accuracy, logical gaps, and unstated assumptions.

commands.name: Red Team
commands.description: Deliberately try to poke holes in the document's argument, find missing edge cases, or identify claims without evidence.

commands.name: Harmonize
commands.description: Ensure consistent terminology, formatting, tone, and style across a multi-author or multi-section document.

commands.name: Version
commands.description: Tag a document revision with a version identifier and change summary. Maintain history without cluttering the current draft.

commands.name: Format
commands.description: Apply the target format — Markdown, DOCX, PDF, Confluence wiki, slide deck, email. Structure meets medium.

commands.name: Finalize
commands.description: Lock the document — no more edits. Mark as approved, add signatures or sign-offs if required.

commands.name: Distribute
commands.description: Deliver the document to its audience — email, wiki publish, SharePoint upload, Slack post, PR comment.

commands.name: Archive
commands.description: Move a superseded or completed document to long-term storage with metadata for future retrieval.

commands.name: Capability Map
commands.description: Identify and organize what the organization does (not how) — business capabilities, technology capabilities, data capabilities. The "what" before the "how."

commands.name: Reference Architecture
commands.description: Define the canonical pattern for a domain — "this is how we build API services" or "this is our identity architecture." Reusable, opinionated, versioned.

commands.name: Technology Radar
commands.description: Evaluate and classify technologies as Adopt / Trial / Assess / Hold. Guides teams without mandating — "we recommend X, we're evaluating Y, stop using Z."

commands.name: Fitness Function
commands.description: Define automated checks that verify an architecture property is being maintained — "all services must respond in <200ms" or "no direct database access from the UI layer."

commands.name: Trade-off Analysis
commands.description: Explicitly document what you gain and what you lose with each architectural decision. No free lunches — make the trade-offs visible.

commands.name: Current-State / Future-State
commands.description: Map where you are today and where you're going. The gap between them is the roadmap.

commands.name: Domain Boundary
commands.description: Define where one domain ends and another begins — bounded contexts, service boundaries, team ownership lines.

commands.name: Portfolio Rationalize
commands.description: Evaluate the existing technology portfolio and decide what to keep, consolidate, migrate, or retire.

commands.name: Trust Model
commands.description: Define who trusts whom, how trust is established, and what trust means in terms of access. The foundation of any identity architecture.

commands.name: Identity Fabric
commands.description: Design the topology of identity providers, directories, and federation relationships. How identity flows across the organization.

commands.name: Protocol Select
commands.description: Choose the right auth protocol for the context — SAML for legacy SSO, OIDC for modern apps, RADIUS for network, Kerberos for on-prem AD, FIDO2 for passwordless.

commands.name: Access Pattern Model
commands.description: Map how users, services, and devices actually access resources — request paths, auth flows, token lifecycles, session management.

commands.name: Entitlement Schema
commands.description: Design the structure of permissions — RBAC roles, ABAC attributes, policy hierarchies, inheritance rules, separation-of-duty constraints.

commands.name: Delegation Model
commands.description: Define how administrative authority is distributed — OU delegation, administrative units, tiered admin, privilege scoping.

commands.name: Identity Lifecycle
commands.description: Design the end-to-end journey from identity creation to decommission — joiner flows, mover workflows, leaver automation, dormant account handling.

commands.name: Authentication Assurance
commands.description: Define assurance levels (e.g., NIST AAL 1/2/3) and map them to authentication methods, step-up triggers, and risk thresholds.

commands.name: Policy Hierarchy
commands.description: Define the structure of policies — enterprise policy → standard → procedure → guideline. Each level constrains the one below it.

commands.name: Decision Rights
commands.description: Specify who can approve what — data classification owners, system owners, risk acceptors, exception approvers. RACI for governance.

commands.name: Control Framework
commands.description: Select or build the control structure — NIST CSF, CIS Controls, ISO 27001, COBIT, or a custom hybrid. Map controls to risks.

commands.name: Risk Taxonomy
commands.description: Categorize risks consistently — operational, compliance, financial, reputational, technical. Enables apples-to-apples comparison across domains.

commands.name: Risk Register
commands.description: Maintain a living inventory of identified risks with likelihood, impact, owner, and treatment plan. The central governance artifact.

commands.name: Separation of Duties
commands.description: Design constraints that prevent any single identity from having conflicting privileges — approve-and-execute, create-and-review.

commands.name: Policy-as-Code
commands.description: Express governance rules in executable form — OPA/Rego, Sentinel, Cedar, AWS SCPs. Shift from document-based to enforceable policy.

commands.name: Topology Design
commands.description: Define the physical and logical layout of the network — hub-and-spoke, mesh, segmented zones, overlay networks.

commands.name: Zone Model
commands.description: Classify network segments by trust level and data sensitivity — DMZ, internal, restricted, management, guest. Traffic rules follow zone boundaries.

commands.name: Traffic Flow Model
commands.description: Map how data actually moves — north-south (in/out), east-west (lateral), service-to-service, user-to-service. Identifies chokepoints and exposure.

commands.name: Connectivity Architecture
commands.description: Design how sites, clouds, and partners connect — SD-WAN, MPLS, VPN tunnels, peering, direct connect. The physical underlay.

commands.name: Service Mesh
commands.description: Define the communication layer between services — sidecar proxies, mTLS, traffic management, observability, retry/circuit-break at the network level.

commands.name: DNS Architecture
commands.description: Design name resolution — split-horizon, conditional forwarding, private zones, service discovery. The invisible plumbing everything depends on.

commands.name: Certificate Architecture
commands.description: Design the PKI hierarchy — root CA, intermediate CAs, certificate lifecycle, auto-renewal, pinning strategy. Trust anchors for the entire infrastructure.

commands.name: Platform Blueprint
commands.description: Define the target developer platform — orchestrator (K8s), runtime, CI/CD, observability, secrets, networking. The "golden path" for teams.

commands.name: Workload Pattern
commands.description: Classify workloads by their operational characteristics — stateless web, stateful database, batch job, event-driven, long-running daemon. Pattern determines topology.

commands.name: Orchestrate
commands.description: Design how containers are scheduled, scaled, health-checked, and replaced — K8s deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs.

commands.name: Image Pipeline
commands.description: Design the container image lifecycle — base image curation, build, scan, sign, promote, deploy. Supply chain security for containers.

commands.name: Infrastructure-as-Code
commands.description: Define infrastructure declaratively — Terraform/HCL, Pulumi, CloudFormation, Crossplane. Version-controlled, reviewable, reproducible.

commands.name: Sidecar / Init Pattern
commands.description: Design helper containers that augment the main workload — secret injection, log forwarding, proxy, certificate refresh.

commands.name: Namespace Strategy
commands.description: Define how K8s namespaces map to teams, environments, or applications. Isolation, resource quotas, RBAC scoping.

commands.name: GitOps
commands.description: Use Git as the single source of truth for infrastructure and application state. Pull-based reconciliation — ArgoCD, Flux.

commands.name: Anchor Drift
commands.description: Agent fixates on an early assumption and keeps building on it even as evidence mounts that it's wrong. Sunk-cost reasoning.
commands.detection_signal: Output keeps referencing an early decision despite contradicting evidence. Agent resists Replan.

commands.name: Premature Converge
commands.description: Agent locks onto the first plausible solution without exploring alternatives. The opposite of Self-Consistency.
commands.detection_signal: Solution appears suspiciously fast. No alternatives mentioned. No trade-off analysis.

commands.name: Hallucinate Confidence
commands.description: Agent presents uncertain information with high confidence. Authoritative tone masks shaky reasoning.
commands.detection_signal: Claims without citations. Specific numbers/dates that weren't in the source material. "Certainly" and "definitely" on ambiguous topics.

commands.name: Cargo Cult
commands.description: Agent copies a pattern it's seen work elsewhere without understanding *why*, applying it where it doesn't fit.
commands.detection_signal: Boilerplate that doesn't match the context. "Best practice" applied without considering constraints.

commands.name: Sycophant Loop
commands.description: Agent agrees with the human's framing even when it's wrong, reinforcing a bad approach instead of pushing back.
commands.detection_signal: Agent never challenges assumptions. Every suggestion is "great idea." Errors in the human's premise go unquestioned.

commands.name: Confabulation Cascade
commands.description: Agent hallucinates a fact, then builds reasoning on top of it, then cites its own earlier reasoning as evidence. Self-reinforcing hallucination.
commands.detection_signal: Conclusions that trace back to unsourced claims from earlier in the same session.

commands.name: Yak Shave
commands.description: Agent gets trapped in a dependency chain: to do A needs B, needs C, needs D... three levels deep solving the wrong problem.
commands.detection_signal: Agent's current task has no visible connection to the original goal. Multiple "first I need to..." transitions.

commands.name: Scope Gallop
commands.description: Each turn subtly expands beyond the original ask. Task triples in size without explicit approval.
commands.detection_signal: Deliverable keeps growing. Agent adds features/tests/docs nobody asked for. "While I'm at it..." language.

commands.name: Gold Plate
commands.description: Agent over-engineers beyond what was asked — adds tests nobody requested, refactors adjacent code, writes docs for a one-off script.
commands.detection_signal: Time/tokens spent is disproportionate to task complexity. Output has polish nobody needs.

commands.name: Infinite Clarify
commands.description: Agent keeps asking clarifying questions instead of making a reasonable assumption and proceeding. Paralysis by over-specification.
commands.detection_signal: Multiple turns of questions before any work output. Questions about details that don't affect the approach.

commands.name: Rabbit Hole
commands.description: Agent goes deep into an interesting tangent that's technically related but not on the critical path. Intellectual curiosity overrides task focus.
commands.detection_signal: Long, detailed output on a subtopic. Agent seems engaged but isn't making progress toward the goal.

commands.name: Ghost Context
commands.description: Information the agent *should* have but doesn't — it was in a previous session, different agent's context, or the human's head.
commands.detection_signal: Agent asks about something that was already discussed. Agent re-derives information that was previously established.

commands.name: Context Poisoning
commands.description: Bad information enters the context (wrong file, outdated doc, hallucinated fact) and corrupts all downstream reasoning.
commands.detection_signal: Conclusions that logically follow from the context but are factually wrong. Agent confidently builds on incorrect premises.

commands.name: Stale Plan
commands.description: Foreman keeps executing the original plan after the situation has changed. The plan was right when made; it's wrong now.
commands.detection_signal: Tasks being executed that no longer make sense. Agent outputs that don't account for recent discoveries.

commands.name: Echo Chamber
commands.description: In multi-agent setups, agents reinforce each other's errors because they share context or were seeded with the same assumptions.
commands.detection_signal: Multiple agents arrive at the same wrong conclusion. Disagreement is suspiciously absent.

commands.name: Context Thrash
commands.description: Agent repeatedly loads, evicts, and reloads the same context because it can't decide what's relevant. Burns tokens without progress.
commands.detection_signal: High token usage with low output quality. Repeated references to the same files/concepts.

commands.name: Deadlock
commands.description: Two or more agents are each waiting for the other to finish before they can proceed. No progress.
commands.detection_signal: Multiple agents show "blocked" status simultaneously. No task state changes over multiple Poll cycles.

commands.name: Thundering Herd
commands.description: Multiple agents simultaneously attempt the same action (API call, file write, resource acquisition), causing contention or failure.
commands.detection_signal: Concurrent modification errors. Rate limit hits. Merge conflicts.

commands.name: Silent Failure
commands.description: Agent fails but doesn't report it — continues with partial results or quietly skips the failed step.
commands.detection_signal: Output is subtly incomplete. Missing sections nobody noticed. Downstream agents working with partial data.

commands.name: Authority Confusion
commands.description: Multiple agents believe they own the same resource or decision. Conflicting edits, contradictory outputs.
commands.detection_signal: Same file modified by multiple agents. Conflicting instructions given to downstream agents.

commands.name: Zombie Agent
commands.description: Agent appears alive (Heartbeat passes) but isn't making meaningful progress — spinning, looping, or producing garbage.
commands.detection_signal: Heartbeat OK but no task state changes. Token usage high but no useful output.

commands.name: Rubber Duck
commands.description: Explain the problem to the agent not because you need its answer, but because articulating the problem reveals the solution. The agent is a forcing function for your own thinking.
commands.when_to_use: When you're stuck and can't see the path forward. The act of writing the prompt IS the work.

commands.name: Shotgun Debug
commands.description: Throw the entire error log, stack trace, and surrounding context at the agent with no decomposition. Just raw context dump + "figure it out." Works surprisingly often.
commands.when_to_use: When the error is opaque and you don't know where to start. Let the agent's pattern matching work.

commands.name: Warm-Up Prompt
commands.description: Give the agent a low-stakes task in the domain before the real task — "summarize this codebase" before "refactor this module." Builds context and calibrates the agent's mental model.
commands.when_to_use: Before complex tasks in unfamiliar codebases. The warm-up is cheap; the calibration is valuable.

commands.name: Inversion Prompt
commands.description: Instead of asking "how should I build X," ask "what are all the ways X could fail?" The failure analysis reveals the design requirements.
commands.when_to_use: Architecture and design phases. The failure modes ARE the specification.

commands.name: Breadcrumb Trail
commands.description: Leave deliberate markers in your code/docs that future agents will find and follow — comments like `// AGENT: this connects to the auth service via OIDC` or `<!-- CONTEXT: this decision was made because of HIPAA §164.312 -->`.
commands.when_to_use: Any codebase where agents will work repeatedly. Invest now, save context-loading costs forever.

commands.name: Throwaway Agent
commands.description: Spawn an agent specifically to explore a dead-end approach, knowing you'll discard the output. The value is confirming "that path doesn't work" so you can commit to the right one.
commands.when_to_use: When two approaches seem equally viable and you need to eliminate one. Cheaper than debating.

commands.name: Shadow Agent
commands.description: Run a second agent on the same task independently with different context or model. Use divergence between outputs as a quality signal.
commands.when_to_use: High-stakes tasks where correctness matters more than speed. The diff between two independent solutions reveals blind spots.

commands.name: Speculative Execute
commands.description: Start work on a likely-needed task before it's officially assigned, betting the foreman will need it. CPU branch prediction for agents.
commands.when_to_use: When the dependency graph is predictable. If the bet pays off, you saved wall-clock time. If not, discard is cheap.

commands.name: Consensus Fork
commands.description: When agents disagree, don't pick a winner — fork the pipeline and pursue both approaches in parallel until one proves itself through testing or validation.
commands.when_to_use: When the disagreement is about approach, not facts. Let reality pick the winner instead of debate.

commands.name: Context Handwarmer
commands.description: Pre-load an agent with relevant context before assigning its task, so when the task arrives, the agent is already "warm" and doesn't waste tokens on discovery.
commands.when_to_use: When you know what tasks are coming and can prepare agents ahead of time. Batch the context loading.

commands.name: Progressive Disclosure
commands.description: Give the agent minimal context first, let it ask for what it needs, and inject on demand. The opposite of Prefill. Reduces noise.
commands.when_to_use: Exploratory tasks where you don't know what context will be relevant. Let the agent's questions guide the context.

commands.name: Canary Prompt
commands.description: Before running a prompt at scale (e.g., `/batch` across 50 files), test it on 2-3 representative files first to verify the output quality and catch prompt bugs.
commands.when_to_use: Before any batch operation. The prompt is code — test it before deploying.

commands.name: Token Budget
commands.description: Explicitly allocate a token budget to a task and treat it like money. Agent must complete within budget or request an extension with justification.
commands.when_to_use: Long-running tasks, expensive models, cost-sensitive environments. Forces efficiency and prevents runaway sessions.

commands.name: Entropy Check
commands.description: Measure how "confused" an agent is by looking at reasoning quality, response length, confidence signals, and number of self-corrections. High entropy = struggling.
commands.when_to_use: During the foreman's Poll cycle. High entropy is an early warning to intervene before the agent wastes tokens.

commands.name: Temporal Fence
commands.description: A constraint that changes over time — "before Friday this is a draft, after Friday it's locked." Time-aware guard rails that evolve with the project lifecycle.
commands.when_to_use: Governance workflows, release management, compliance deadlines. The rules change based on where you are in the timeline.

commands.name: Negative Space
commands.description: Deliberately define what the agent should NOT explore, NOT touch, NOT change. The absence of action is itself a design decision.
commands.when_to_use: Any task near production systems, sensitive data, or shared infrastructure. "Do not modify anything in `/prod`" is as important as the task itself.

commands.name: Perspective Shift
commands.description: Ask the agent to re-evaluate its own output from a different persona's viewpoint — "now review this as a security auditor" or "would a junior developer understand this?"
commands.when_to_use: After draft completion. Cheap way to catch blind spots without spawning a separate review agent.

commands.name: Exit Criteria
commands.description: Define upfront exactly what "done" looks like AND what "good enough" looks like. Two thresholds — the agent aims for "done" but can stop at "good enough" if budget is tight.
commands.when_to_use: Every task. Without Exit Criteria, agents either Gold Plate or deliver incomplete work. Spec's acceptance criteria should include both thresholds.
